item.title: Prime Bank, Angelica International School sign PrimeAcademia MoU to enhance educational banking
item.description: Prime Bank PLC has recently entered into a strategic partnership with Angelica International School (AIS) under its flagship educational initiative, PrimeAcademia. This collaboration aims to provide a sophisticated suite of specialized financial services tailored for the AIS community, according t

item.title: 13th JS: Orientation meeting held to brief whips
item.description: An orientation meeting was held on Wednesday to brief the Chief Whip and Whips of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad on the activities and functions of the National Parliament and the Parliament Secretariat. The special meeting took place at the Cabinet Room of the Bangladesh Parliament Secretariat in Dhaka,

item.title: Adidas store in Israel hit by bomb, says chief commercial officer
item.description: Adidas’ chief commercial officer said on Wednesday that one of the company’s franchise stores in Israel had been hit by a bomb, but no one had been injured, amid the unfolding war in Iran. “We had one franchise store in Israel which was hit indeed by an attack three days ago,&rdq

item.title: Britain needs to keep a cool head over Middle East, UK PM Starmer says
item.description: Britain ​needs to keep a cool head ‌over the conflict in the Middle East to address the public's concerns about escalation, ​Prime Minister Keir Starmer said ​on Wednesday. Starmer was speaking after US President ⁠Donald Trump heaped fresh criticism on his leadership ​over Britain's limited s

item.title: PM asks Ansar, VDP to stay vigilance to protect sovereignty
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday directed the members of Bangladesh Ansar and Village Defence Party (VDP) to maintain the highest preparedness to protect the country’s sovereignty alongside ensuring peace and security. The premier gave the directive when Ansar and VDP Director Gene

item.title: PM orders intensifying nationwide tree plantation programme
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman today (Wednesday) issued a directive for intensifying the tree plantation programme across the country. The premier gave the directive while he was presiding over a meeting of the cell formed over planting 25 crore trees throughout the next five years, BSS reports.&nb

item.title: Female teacher stabbed to death by employee at Islamic University in Kushtia
item.description: A female teacher has been stabbed to death in her office at Islamic University in Kushtia, allegedly by an employee of the same department. University Proctor Shahinuzzaman told journalists that Asma Sadiya Runa, an assistant professor and chair of the social welfare department, was killed in the

item.title: PM to launch Farmer Card distribution on April 14
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman is expected to launch the Farmer Card formally on a pilot basis on April 14 on the occasion of the first day of the Bangla New Year 1433. The decision was taken at a review meeting held at the Cabinet Division Conference Room in the Bangladesh Secretariat here today w

item.title: UNDP reaffirms support for Bangladesh’s climate, development priorities
item.description: UNDP Resident Representative in Bangladesh Stefan Liller on Wednesday reaffirmed UNDP’s support for the Government of Bangladesh’s climate and development priorities. He met Environment, Forest and Climate Change Minister Abdul Awal Mintoo and discussed areas of ongoing cooperation. T

item.title: 46 flights cancelled at Chattogram airport in 5 days
item.description: Authorities have cancelled a total of 46 flights over the past five days between Chattogram and Middle East destinations due to the Iran war. By Wednesday afternoon, 11 flights -- six outbound and five inbound -- were scrapped at Shah Amanat International Airport, according to spokesperson Ibrahim

item.title: BB postpones MPC meeting on rate cut as economist Sadiq Ahmed resigns
item.description: Bangladesh Bank (BB) on Wednesday postponed a crucial meeting of its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) following the resignation of noted economist Dr. Sadiq Ahmed from the committee. The meeting, chaired by the newly appointed Governor, Md. Mostaqur Rahman was widely expected to announce a reductio

item.title: Over 4m submit tax returns online
item.description: More than 4 million taxpayers have submitted their income tax returns online for the 2025–26 tax year so far, reflecting a sharp rise in the use of the electronic filing system, according to the National Board of Revenue (NBR). In a press release issued on Wednesday, the revenue authority sa
